prompt_text: Finaloop vs QuickBooks pricing and features
response:
# Finaloop vs QuickBooks: Pricing & Features

## Pricing Overview

| Aspect | Finaloop | QuickBooks |
|--------|----------|-----------|
| **Starting Price** | Free plan available | $15-$235/month |
| **Free Trial** | Yes (14 days) | Yes (30 days) |
| **Model** | Freemium + paid tiers | Subscription only |

### Finaloop Pricing Tiers
- **Free**: Basic features, limited transactions
- **Starter**: ~$15-30/month
- **Professional**: ~$50-100/month
- **Enterprise**: Custom pricing

### QuickBooks Pricing Tiers
- **Simple Start**: $15/month (invoicing + expenses)
- **Essentials**: $55/month (+ bill pay)
- **Plus**: $99/month (+ inventory)
- **Advanced**: $235/month (+ analytics)

---

## Key Features Comparison

### Accounting & Bookkeeping
| Feature | Finaloop | QuickBooks |
|---------|----------|-----------|
| Invoicing | ✅ | ✅ |
| Expense Tracking | ✅ | ✅ |
| Bank Integration | ✅ | ✅ |
| Receipt Scanning | ✅ | ✅ (Plus+) |
| Multi-currency | ✅ | ✅ (varies) |
| Bill Management | Limited | ✅ |
| Payroll | ❌ | ✅ (extra) |

### Automation & Intelligence
- **Finaloop**: AI-powered expense categorization, receipt recognition
- **QuickBooks**: Workflow automation, custom reports, mobile-first design

### Integration Support
- **Finaloop**: 200+ integrations
- **QuickBooks**: 400+ integrations (broader ecosystem)

---

## Best For

**Finaloop:**
- Freelancers & startups on tight budgets
- Expense-focused businesses
- Users wanting simplicity over full bookkeeping
- Teams needing AI-powered receipt scanning

**QuickBooks:**
- Growing small-to-medium businesses
- Companies needing payroll features
- US tax compliance critical
- Advanced reporting requirements

---

## Bottom Line

| Winner | Category |
|--------|----------|
| **Finaloop** | Best budget option, AI features |
| **QuickBooks** | Best all-in-one solution, payroll |
| **Finaloop** | Easier learning curve |
| **QuickBooks** | Better tax support (US) |

**Choose Finaloop** if cost and simplicity matter most.
**Choose QuickBooks** if you need comprehensive small business accounting.

Would you like deeper details on any specific feature?
